title: Internal tracker-ID citations in code comments are opaque jargon to a reader outside this project, independent of whether the ID still resolves
description: A code comment that cites this project's internal tracker IDs (FEAT-NNN, TASK-NNN, DEC-NNNN, FGAP-NNN, VER-NNN, JI-NNN, etc.) is opaque to any reader without access to the active .context substrate -- a library consumer reading the published @davidorex/pi-agent-dispatch source, or a new contributor. This holds whether or not the cited ID still resolves to the correct current item: a comment citing a currently-valid ID (e.g. FGAP-134, DEC-0023) is exactly as unreadable to that outside reader as one citing a dangling ID, because the reader has no way to look either one up. Staleness (the concern issue-012 originally named) is a distinct, narrower defect from opacity (this gap): a citation can be perfectly accurate and still be jargon-noise. citation-rot-scanner.ts (packages/pi-context/src/citation-rot-scanner.ts) already forbids these ID-shaped tokens from shipped operator-facing surfaces (tool descriptions, thrown error messages, README/markdown bodies, JSON description fields) but its own test suite confirms it deliberately excludes JSDoc and line comments in source -- comments are assumed to be internal-provenance notes, not something that needs policing. scan-comment-citations.ts (TASK-107) enumerates citations in comments but is informational only, not a gate.

evidence.reference: CITATION_RE / scanTsFile scope operator-visible surfaces only (registerTool/Type.X description strings, new *Error() messages, JSON/markdown/yaml bodies); its own test 'does NOT flag JSDoc comments referencing canonical_ids' confirms comments are an intentional exclusion, not an oversight
TASK-107's new tool enumerates every comment-embedded citation across all 7 packages (588 instances, 54 files) but exits 0 unconditionally -- informational, not enforced

header comment cites FEAT-004/DEC-0044/FEAT-005/TASK-081; all four are genuine, technically-accurate citations to the .project-archived predecessor substrate's items, verified this session -- yet every one is unreadable to a reader without that archive, independent of the fact they also happen to collide with unrelated live-substrate IDs of the same number
prior investigation that surfaced issue-012's specific stale-ID instance, narrower than this class

impact: Every code comment in this repo's published packages that cites a bare internal tracker ID communicates nothing to a reader outside this project's own .context substrate, regardless of the ID's accuracy. This degrades comment quality across all shipped source (this session's sweep of packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/*.ts alone found 25 distinct ID citations across 24 files), and the defect recurs every time a comment is authored citing an ID instead of stating its point in plain English -- there is no gate that catches a newly-introduced instance.
proposed_resolution: Comments should state the substantive engineering point in plain English instead of a bare ID pointer -- an ID is a pointer for internal PM tracking, not a substitute for explaining the reasoning inline. Eventual enforcement: extend citation-rot-scanner.ts's detection surface to also cover code comments (never fork a second parallel gate -- scan-comment-citations.ts already had to hand-duplicate part of its regex because the scanner's CITATION_RE is unexported with no package subpath), delta-scoped so only a NEWLY-INTRODUCED comment citation in a diff is flagged (per this project's established delta-scoped write-time-invariant pattern) -- a full-repo error-severity gate today would immediately block on the ~500+ pre-existing instances found across all 7 packages. This gate is filed as future work, not built as part of this gap's triggering-instance fix (TASK-108, which rewrites pi-agent-dispatch's own comments as the first concrete instance of the standard).
